regimeNotes: NEUTRAL regime maintained 2026-07-03 (Friday, holiday-shortened session). June nonfarm payrolls shocked to the downside: +57K vs 110-113K consensus (unemployment 4.2%); April/May prior revised down further. CME odds of a July 29 hike collapsed to ~22% (hold now overwhelming favorite), though September hike odds remain elevated (~60%) per some desks -- net effect is a modestly less-hawkish near-term path, not a dovish pivot. Fed Chair Warsh called the labor picture 'steady' and kept emphasizing the 2% inflation target -- no capitulation signal from the Fed itself. 10Y yield ~4.47-4.49%, little changed. DXY eased to ~100.8 (from 101.2) on the soft jobs print. Equity action was mixed/rotational, not a clean risk-on breadth expansion: Dow +1.1% to a fresh record, S&P roughly flat, Nasdaq -0.8% on continued chip-sector softness (AVGO -2.4%, NVDA -1.4%); META plunged -4.9% (cause not fully confirmed by evening reports, treated as company-specific risk, not sector signal). Gold ripped +2.0% (GLD 378.13; spot ~$4,150) on the growth-scare/dovish-repricing combo -- already a large, extended move over the past month, so read as a hedge doing its job rather than a fresh entry opportunity. Crypto: total mcap $2.22T (+1.19% 24h), BTC dominance 55.58% (stable), Fear & Greed 21 (Extreme Fear, now 3+ weeks running) -- sentiment still washed out despite BTC/ETH/SOL all posting modest gains overnight. Crypto sleeve now 19.56% of NAV, essentially at the 20% neutral-regime cap -- no capacity to add without a regime upgrade or a trim elsewhere. Cash 13.66% of NAV, comfortably above the 5% floor. NAV 98,876.73 (+0.43% vs prior close). REGIME UNCHANGED (neutral): soft labor print does not by itself flip the equity/crypto breadth picture (tech/semis still soft, F&G still Extreme Fear); DXY/yield moves are incremental, not a trend reversal. Researched META (unclear single-day catalyst, no informational edge to act on) and BRK-B (Berkshire's ~$400B cash pile and active net equity selling under Abel reads as a valuation caution signal from the operator itself, not a buy signal; PEG ~10 is rich) -- neither clears the bar. No invalidation levels breached on any holding. HOLD day, zero trades: every thesis intact, crypto sleeve capped, no new name shows a clear data-backed edge after today's information.

SPY.thesis: Core US equity beta at $744.78 (above $741.75 avgCost; +0.41%, ~13.20% NAV). Dow at record highs, S&P resilient though flat today; broad index still the diversified anchor while breadth stays uneven under the surface (chip/megacap tech softness). Hold.
SPY.invalidationLevel: Sustained close below 660 USD or confirmed multi-hike path forcing a technical downtrend in the broad index.

NVDA.thesis: AI/compute secular leader at $194.83 (below $208.44 avgCost; -6.53%, ~13.71% NAV). Down again with chip-sector softness (AVGO -2.4% same session) but AI capex cycle and data-center demand narrative intact; no rollover evidence yet. HOLD, monitor for further semis weakness.
NVDA.invalidationLevel: AI capex digestion or data-center demand rollover; close below 150 USD.

MSFT.thesis: Quality AI/cloud compounder at $390.49 (near 391.98 avgCost; -0.38%, ~15.04% NAV). At the 15% position cap -- no further adds. Azure + Copilot growth thesis unchanged. HOLD.
MSFT.invalidationLevel: Cloud growth deceleration or AI capex without demonstrable ROI; close below 340 USD.

GOOGL.thesis: Quality growth (search + cloud + AI) at $359.91 (below $366.53 avgCost; -1.81%, ~14.30% NAV). Search monopoly thesis intact; AI integration ongoing. HOLD, near cap.
GOOGL.invalidationLevel: Search share erosion to AI rivals causing revenue decline; close below 310 USD.

UNH.thesis: Defensive value at $425.36 (above $408.52 avgCost; +4.12%, ~6.32% NAV). Healthcare defensive ballast, small position acting as a natural hedge. HOLD.
UNH.invalidationLevel: Further regulatory or medical-cost shock; close below 360 USD.

GLD.thesis: Tail/debasement hedge at $378.13 (below $386.54 avgCost; -2.18%, ~4.22% NAV). Gold ripped to ~$4,150 spot on the soft-jobs/dovish-repricing combo -- the hedge is doing its job, but the move is already extended over the past month so not adding here (chasing risk). HOLD.
GLD.invalidationLevel: Real yields spike with strong USD reversal sustained; close below 350 USD.

BTC.thesis: Core crypto sleeve leader at $61,612 (below $64,629 avgCost; -4.67%, ~13.98% NAV). Modest overnight gain despite F&G still at 21 (Extreme Fear, 3+ weeks). BTC dominance 55.58% stable. Crypto sleeve at 19.56% -- essentially at the 20% neutral cap, cannot add. HOLD; await regime improvement or sleeve room.

ETH.thesis: Discounted L1 leader at $1,730.88 (above 1,667.47 avgCost; +3.80%, ~4.20% NAV). Back above average cost on continued crypto recovery. Asymmetric upside intact when risk-on returns and crypto cap expands to 35%. HOLD.
ETH.invalidationLevel: Structural loss of share to L1 rivals; close below 1,300 USD.

SOL.thesis: High-beta satellite at $80.92 (above 67.81 avgCost; +19.34%, ~1.38% NAV). Continuing to outperform peers in the crypto recovery. Crypto sleeve at cap -- no room to add. HOLD small position for further upside on regime flip.
SOL.invalidationLevel: Break below 52 USD; network deterioration or sentiment collapse.
